channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Federal Agency Deploys Cybersecurity Sensors in Northern Territories to Combat Cyber Threats
item.description: The Communications Security Establishment (CSE) has deployed cybersecurity sensors across northern Canada's territorial government networks, aiming to detect and prevent malicious cyber activity. The ...

item.title: 18th-Century Shipwreck Uncovered with Well-Preserved 18th-Century Cargo
item.description: A remarkable 18th-century shipwreck was discovered in the Skagerrak Strait off Norway's southeastern coast, containing an exceptionally well-preserved cargo of undamaged Chinese porcelain, chandeliers...

item.title: Rachel Ruto’s Female Bodyguard Commended for Vigilance at Wedding
item.description: First Lady Rachel Ruto attended a high-profile wedding reception where she was filmed dancing through the crowd in a vibrant patterned outfit. Her female bodyguard, dressed in a black suit, stayed glu...

item.title: Lindsey Graham Dies After Ignoring Medical Advice, Report Indicates
item.description: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) died of a sudden illness, according to his office, which cited a 'brief and sudden illness' as the cause. A new Axios report suggests Graham dismissed medical advice...

item.title: Iran and US escalate Gulf conflict over control of vital shipping route
item.description: Iran and the US have escalated military tensions in the Gulf, with Iran launching missile and drone strikes against US facilities across the region. The attacks, which include targeting Qatar and the ...

item.title: Celebrities attend England vs Norway World Cup game: Stars cheer on Three Lions
item.description: The article details the attendance of various celebrities at the England vs Norway World Cup quarter-final match. Notable figures such as Jude Bellingham's girlfriend Ashlyn Castro, Harry Kane's wife ...

item.title: Philippines reinforces role in upholding international maritime law through defense partnerships
item.description: The Department of National Defense (DND) Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. emphasized the Philippines' emergence as a global convergence hub for upholding international law, particularly the United Natio...

item.title: Conor McGregor Sustains Leg Injury in UFC Return, Ends Fight After 1 Minute
item.description: Conor McGregor's highly anticipated return to the UFC ended in disaster after sustaining a severe leg injury just 1 minute into his main event bout against Max Holloway at UFC 329. The former two-divi...

item.title: Understanding the Limitations and Cost-Benefit of Crypto Escrow Services
item.description: This article examines the practical limitations of crypto escrow services, emphasizing that while they provide security for payments, they do not guarantee quality or the integrity of the counterparty...

item.title: NRC Proposes NEPA Rule Changes to Accelerate Nuclear Licensing
item.description: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has proposed significant revisions to its National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) rules, aiming to modernize its environmental review framework for nuclear lic...

item.title: ONR Extends Compliance Deadlines for Asbestos Management at Torness Nuclear Plant
item.description: The Office for Nuclear Regulation (ONR) has extended compliance deadlines for two improvement notices related to asbestos management at the Torness Nuclear Power Station in Scotland. The incident occu...

item.title: Tornado damages structures and oil sites in Alberta hamlet, officials report
item.description: A tornado struck the east-central Alberta hamlet of Tulliby Lake, causing damage to several structures, oil sites, and local vegetation. County officials from Vermilion River confirmed the event in a ...

item.title: Iran's Lost Literary Voice: Shahrnush Parsipur's Legacy in Persian Literature
item.description: Shahrnush Parsipur, an Iranian feminist writer and novelist, passed away at 80, leaving behind a legacy that transformed modern Persian literature. Over five decades, she challenged societal norms by ...

item.title: No Winners in Major Lotto Draws on July 12, 2026
item.description: On Sunday, July 12, 2026, there were no winners for the major lotto games held by the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PSCO). The Ultra Lotto 6/58 game's jackpot remained at P128,000,000.00 as n...

item.title: Common Causes of Nighttime Urination and How to Manage Them
item.description: Nighttime urination, or nocturia, is a common issue affecting many people's quality of life. Experts suggest that factors such as pregnancy, excessive water intake before bed, and age-related conditio...

item.title: Mitra Samal's Poetry Explores Memory, Desire, and Emotional Intimacy
item.description: Mitra Samal's poetry collection 'Silence Has a Sound' delves into themes of memory, desire, and emotional intimacy through five thematic sections. The poems, often set in domestic spheres, explore per...

item.title: El Niño's Impact on South Africa's Weather and Agriculture
item.description: A strong El Niño climate pattern is developing in the Pacific Ocean, with a 90% chance of persisting until November. This phenomenon is expected to bring extreme heat, drought, and veld fires across S...

item.title: Van der Poel clinches Tour de France stage nine victory as Pogacar extends lead
item.description: Mathieu van der Poel secured his third career Tour de France stage win on Sunday after a dramatic finish to stage nine, which saw him triumph in a chaotic scramble for the line. The 31-year-old Dutch ...

item.title: Tanzania Warns Against Fake Pesticides Threatening Farmers' Livelihoods
item.description: Tanzanian authorities and agricultural experts are alarmed by the spread of counterfeit pesticides in local markets, comparing sellers of fake agricultural inputs to murderers due to their devastating...

item.title: Federal Government Raises WAEC and NECO Exam Fees to ₦50,000 Starting 2027
item.description: The Federal Government has approved a new examination fee of ₦50,000 for WAEC and NECO candidates from 2027, nearly doubling the current rates. This decision has sparked widespread criticism from stud...
